title: Diagnose and fix storage initialization failure, then add ads.txt endpoint

requirements.text: Add comprehensive diagnostic logging to the storage initialization sequence in the frontend to capture: the exact method name being called, the complete error text/code, the canister principal being contacted, whether it's a network/agent error vs canister reject/trap vs permission issue, and the network (IC mainnet) confirmation. Log output should be visible in browser console during page load.

quotes: Add a lightweight diagnostic path (logs only; no UI changes): Log the exact failing call (method name) and error code/message. Log whether the storage canister principal is reachable and responding.

acceptanceCriteria: Diagnostic logs appear in browser console on page load
Logs capture the failing method name and full error text/code
Logs identify whether error is network/agent vs canister reject/trap vs permission issue
Logs confirm which canister ID is being contacted and on which network

requirements.text: Perform automated health checks on the storage system and log results: verify all relevant canister(s) have sufficient cycles, confirm the network is IC mainnet, verify the frontend canister ID bindings used in production are correct and match the deployed backend canister, check for any ACL/auth/CORS configuration issues that could block storage calls, and verify the backend canister is installed, running, and responding to health check queries.

quotes: Run a health check: Verify the relevant canister(s) are installed, running, and have sufficient cycles. Verify any required canister IDs in configuration/env are present and correct for production. Verify permissions/ACLs for write operations (caller principal / anonymous access rules). Verify that the storage service isn't rate-limited or blocked by CORS/agent config.

acceptanceCriteria: Health check confirms cycles balance for each relevant canister
Health check verifies network is IC mainnet
Health check validates frontend canister ID bindings match production backend
Health check identifies any ACL/auth/CORS issues blocking storage access
Health check confirms backend canister is installed and responding

requirements.text: Based on diagnostic findings, apply the appropriate fix to restore storage availability: if cycles are low, top up cycles; if wrong canister ID/config, update frontend to point to correct production canister IDs; if asset upload/storage is failing, redeploy assets; if storage canister is missing/misconfigured, recreate or rebind it. Document the root cause identified (storage architecture used, specific canister IDs, exact failure point) and the fix applied.

quotes: Fix based on what you find: If cycles are low: top up cycles and redeploy. If wrong canister ID/config: point frontend to the correct production canister IDs and redeploy. If asset upload/storage is failing: redeploy assets and confirm asset canister serves files reliably. If a storage canister is missing/misconfigured: recreate or rebind it, migrate minimal state if needed.

acceptanceCriteria: Storage initialization succeeds on page load without errors
The 'Storage temporarily offline' banner no longer appears
Root cause is documented (which canister(s), specific failure, architecture type)
Fix applied is documented (what changed and why it resolves the issue)

requirements.text: After storage is restored, verify functionality by running a simple read/write test: create a small test record in the storage system, then read it back to confirm both write and read operations succeed. The test should execute automatically after deployment and log success/failure results.

quotes: Confirmation steps: After redeploy, confirm the banner is gone. Confirm storage operations succeed by running a simple read/write test (create a small record and read it back).

acceptanceCriteria: Write test successfully creates a small test record in storage
Read test successfully retrieves the saved record
Test results are logged and confirm storage read/write operations work
Banner is confirmed gone after successful storage restoration

requirements.text: Implement a /ads.txt endpoint that serves the exact content 'google.com, pub-7830122739302232, DIRECT, f08c47fec0942fa0' (without quotes) as plain text with proper MIME type (text/plain). The endpoint must be accessible at https://capsulevault-1ie.caffeine.xyz/ads.txt for AdSense publisher verification.

quotes: After storage is stable, implement /ads.txt so this URL works: https://capsulevault-1ie.caffeine.xyz/ads.txt and returns exactly: google.com, pub-7830122739302232, DIRECT, f08c47fec0942fa0

acceptanceCriteria: GET request to /ads.txt returns status 200
Response Content-Type header is text/plain
Response body is exactly: google.com, pub-7830122739302232, DIRECT, f08c47fec0942fa0
Endpoint is accessible at https://capsulevault-1ie.caffeine.xyz/ads.txt

constraints: Do not modify application UI/UX or business logic during diagnostic phase
Do not change the existing capsule types, storage schema, or core functionality
Preserve all existing frontend components in frontend/src/components/ui as read-only
Maintain single-actor backend architecture with all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo
Ensure diagnostics are logging-only with no user-facing changes
Apply fixes only after diagnostic phase identifies root cause
nonGoals: Redesigning the storage architecture or data models
Adding new capsule features or capabilities
Modifying the authentication flow or Internet Identity integration
Changing the UI theme or visual design
Implementing additional AdSense ad placements beyond ads.txt
